issue_title: Unrecoverable state after attempting to log in but failed to create network request

reasoning_trace.finding: Initial ripgrep was unavailable; fallback search found the magic-link login code under Simplenote/src/main/java/com/automattic/simplenote/authentication and magic-link strings including "Requesting log in link".
Likely entry points include SignInFragment.kt, SimplenoteAuthenticationActivity.java, SimplenoteSignupActivity.java, NewCredentialsActivity.kt, and magiclink classes.

Authentication activities are SimplenoteAuthenticationActivity, SimplenoteSignupActivity, and NewCredentialsActivity; DeepLinkActivity handles simplenote login links. This confirms the login UI flow is activity/fragment based.

SignInFragment is the login-with-email entry point. Its action button calls viewModel.requestLogin(email) when NetworkUtils reports connectivity. It observes MagicLinkRequestUiState: Loading shows the progress dialog, Error hides it and shows a toast, Success resets state, hides the dialog, and opens the confirmation screen.
This fragment handles completing an already requested login link/code and shows a different loading message, "Logging In". It is not the source of the reported "Requesting log in link" spinner.
The exact spinner text comes from string resource magic_link_request_login_loading_message in Simplenote/src/main/res/values/strings.xml.
The login email button is @id/button with text @string/magic_link_login. This button is wired by MagicLinkableFragment and overridden by SignInFragment.
magic_link_request_login_loading_message is "Requesting log in link", matching the bug report's persistent spinner.

MagicLinkableFragment wires @id/button to onActionButtonClicked, provides showProgressDialog(label), and hideDialogProgress(). The progress dialog is only dismissed when callers invoke hideDialogProgress.
Tapping "Log in with email" enters RequestMagicLinkViewModel.requestLogin after a coarse connectivity check.
requestLogin posts Loading before invoking magicLinkRepository.requestLogin(username). It posts Success or Error for repository results, but the try/catch only catches IOException.
MagicLinkRepository exposes requestLogin(username) returning MagicLinkResponseResult; the view model depends on this result to leave Loading state.

OkHttpMagicLinkRepository.requestLogin calls simpleHttp.firePostRequest("account/request-login", mapOf(...)).use and converts HTTP success/error responses to MagicLinkResponseResult. Exceptions thrown before a Response is returned are not converted here.

SimpleHttp.firePostRequest builds the URL, builds JSON request body, creates an OkHttp Request, and executes it synchronously. Request construction/body creation can throw unchecked exceptions; execute can throw IOException.
NetworkModule provides OkHttpClient with a 30-second read timeout and SimpleHttp. This supports the path from the view model to OkHttp.
DataModule binds MagicLinkRepository to OkHttpMagicLinkRepository, confirming the concrete implementation used by RequestMagicLinkViewModel.

reasoning_trace.target: failed to create network request|create network request|TimeoutException|AsyncHttpClient|Simperium.WebSocket|request-login|complete-login
reasoning_trace.finding: The exact failure wording is not in app source. Local source only references request-login in OkHttpMagicLinkRepository. TimeoutException appears in screenshot tests and the issue log, not in the magic-link request code.

Tests cover repository success and MagicLinkError result, but do not cover repository exceptions other than returned errors. There is no test ensuring Loading transitions to Error on unchecked request failures.

Completion tests similarly cover success/error result states, not unchecked exceptions. This is relevant because the same narrow IOException pattern exists there, but the reported spinner is from request login.
CompleteMagicLinkViewModel also catches only IOException, but its loading string is "Logging In", not the reported "Requesting log in link". This is a similar risk but not the immediate symptom.
This activity handles deep-linked magic-link completion and WordPress login. It uses CompleteMagicLinkViewModel for KEY_IS_MAGIC_LINK; it is not the initial email-link request screen.
loginWithToken only stores authorized Simperium user data after a completed link. It is downstream of successful completion and unrelated to the request spinner.

isNetworkAvailable only checks active NetworkInfo connected status. It can allow a request attempt even if actual HTTP request creation/execution later fails; it does not provide terminal UI handling.
Signup uses a separate OkHttp enqueue path and explicitly hides the progress dialog in showDialogError/onResponse. It is not the login magic-link request path.
SimplenoteSignupActivity chooses SignInFragment when KEY_IS_LOGIN is true, confirming the reported login email request path enters SignInFragment rather than SignupFragment.

If magicLinkRepository.requestLogin throws any non-IOException while creating/executing the request, the coroutine exits after posting Loading and no Error state is posted. The SignInFragment observer therefore never calls hideDialogProgress.

The dialog code can dismiss an existing ProgressDialogFragment when Error or Success is observed. The persistent spinner requires that neither terminal state be observed, pointing upstream to the view model state transition.
A false-positive connectivity check can permit a failing request, but IOException failures would still be caught and converted to Error. It does not explain an unrecoverable Loading state by itself.
HTTP non-2xx responses are converted to MagicLinkError and would hide the spinner. The failure must be an exception before a MagicLinkResponseResult exists.

reasoning_trace.target: Fault statement is the narrow catch in RequestMagicLinkViewModel.requestLogin
reasoning_trace.finding: The missing handling for non-IOException request failures is the single statement most likely causing the observed permanent "Requesting log in link" spinner after a failed network request creation.

fault_candidates.reasoning: The UI posts Loading before the request and only hides the spinner when Error or Success is posted. This catch handles only IOException, so a failure to create/execute the request that surfaces as a non-IOException, such as an unchecked request construction/runtime failure or TimeoutException-like failure, cancels the coroutine without posting MagicLinkRequestUiState.Error. The fragment therefore never calls hideDialogProgress and the "Requesting log in link" spinner remains indefinitely.

final_answer.causal_chain: User taps the email magic-link login button in SignInFragment. SignInFragment.onActionButtonClicked calls RequestMagicLinkViewModel.requestLogin. requestLogin immediately posts MagicLinkRequestUiState.Loading with magic_link_request_login_loading_message, so SignInFragment shows the "Requesting log in link" progress dialog. The repository call then attempts to create/execute the account/request-login POST request. If that request path fails with a non-IOException before returning MagicLinkResponseResult, the catch block at line 51 does not run. The coroutine terminates without posting MagicLinkRequestUiState.Error or Success. Since SignInFragment hides the progress dialog only in Error or Success observers, the spinner remains forever.
final_answer.fix_hint: Convert all request failures in requestLogin into MagicLinkRequestUiState.Error, either by catching a broader Exception/Throwable around magicLinkRepository.requestLogin or by making the repository wrap request-construction failures into MagicLinkError.
